content.problemStatement:
Chef has scored A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC marks in 333 different subjects respectively.

Chef will fail if the **average** score of any two subjects is less than 353535. Determine whether Chef will pass or fail.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, three integers A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C, denoting the Chef's score in the three subjects.


Output Format
For each test case, if Chef will pass, print `PASS`, otherwise print `FAIL`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `pass`, `Pass`, `pAss`, and `PASS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and C — Chef's scores in the three subjects.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "PASS" if the average of every pair of subjects is at least 35, otherwise output "FAIL".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: sums are 70, 75, 99 → all >=70 → PASS.
Test case 2: 28+36=64 <70 → FAIL.
Test case 3: 0+0=0 <70 → FAIL.
Test case 4: all sums equal 70 → PASS.

solution.approach: For a pair of scores (x, y) the average is (x + y) / 2. The condition average >= 35 is equivalent to x + y >= 70. Therefore Chef passes iff all three sums A+B, A+C and B+C are at least 70. For each test case we compute the three sums and check the condition in O(1) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
